coveredRequirements: Active planning warning uses yellow or red ANSI color codes for high visibility
Active planning warning is enclosed within visible borders (yellow borders or box-drawing chars)
Active planning warning contains the planning session title
Active planning warning includes a recognisable label (e.g. 'active planning')
Edge case: banner renders correctly even with empty title
Edge case: no banner produced when planning session is inactive
Planning state round-trips correctly via applyPlanningState/getPlanningState
ambiguousRequirements: Whether the color should be specifically yellow, red, or configurable – plan says 'yellow or red'; tests accept either
Whether borders should be box-drawing characters or repeated dashes/equals – tests accept both
The exact exported name of the banner formatting function is unknown – tests try multiple plausible names
Whether the banner is rendered as a persistent bottom bar or inline output is unclear
notes: Tests follow the existing node:test + node:assert/strict convention found in the repository examples.
The tests import from extensions/plan-feature.ts and expect a new __testables export for the banner formatting function.
Multiple candidate function names (formatPlanningBanner, renderPlanningWarning, formatActivePlanningMessage, getPlanningBanner) are tried to be resilient to naming choices.
Tests are RED-first: they will fail until the banner formatting function is implemented and exported from __testables.
ANSI detection helpers cover common SGR codes for yellow (33, 93, 38;5;3/11/226/220) and red (31, 91, 38;5;1/9/196).
Border detection covers unicode box-drawing characters and repeated ASCII dashes/equals.
